name: @radzor/oauth-token-refresh
description: Automatic OAuth 2.0 access token refresh and lifecycle management. Handles token expiry detection, background refresh, secure storage, and provider-specific quirks for Google, GitHub, Discord, Stripe, and custom providers.

inputs.name: tokenEndpoint
inputs.description: Provider token endpoint URL. Omit to use a preset provider.

inputs.name: provider
inputs.description: Known provider preset. Sets tokenEndpoint and quirks automatically.

inputs.name: refreshBuffer
inputs.description: Seconds before expiry to proactively refresh the token.

events.name: onRefreshed
events.description: Fired when the access token is successfully refreshed.

events.name: onExpired
events.description: Fired when the refresh token itself has expired. Requires re-authorization.

events.name: onError
events.description: Fired when a refresh attempt fails.

actions.name: getToken

params.name: userId
params.description: User or account ID to retrieve the token for.

actions.description: Return a valid access token, refreshing automatically if near expiry.

actions.name: store

params.name: userId
params.description: User ID to associate with the tokens.

actions.description: Store an initial token set after OAuth authorization.

actions.name: revoke

params.name: userId
params.description: User ID whose tokens should be revoked.

actions.description: Revoke the refresh token and delete stored tokens.

actions.name: isExpired
actions.description: Check if the current access token is expired or about to expire.

llm.constraints: Refresh tokens must be stored securely (encrypted at rest). GitHub tokens do not expire unless the user revokes access. Call store() after the initial OAuth authorization flow (handled by @radzor/auth-oauth). Never log access tokens.
